titleStmt.title: Letter from Samuel Shaw to James Shaw, 29 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Samuel Shaw

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Samuel Shaw to his father, James Shaw, reassuring him that he is well and asking about life back home. Samuel mentions that the boys are all asking after him and that he is clearly better of without Samuel and William helping him with the fishing.
Samuel Henry Shaw (1896-1967) was a sergeant with the 10th Battalion, Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers. He volunteered for active service in September 1914. Before enlisting Samuel was a member of the Portstewart Company, Coleraine Battalion, North Derry Regiment, Ulster Volunteer Force. Samuel served in France, and was wounded during the Battle of the Somme in July 1916 and again in early 1917 and in 1918. Before the war he was employed as a ironmonger's assistant.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Same Address 09.12.15 My Dear
Dad, Just a few lines to
let you know I am well. I received
your letter all right. I am also
glad to see that the fishing is
improving. Tell all the boys at
home I was asking for them. The boys here were all asking for
you, they heard about the good shots
you had & say you are better
without Willie & myself. Our boys
has a saying out here its (you
should know). Keep up your heart.
Hoping to see you all soon. All
at present. I remain
Your Loving Son Sammie P.S. a prosperous
& Happy New
Year
Mr. Jas Shaw
Shorncliffe
Portstewart
Co. Derry
Ireland ;HF Chillingsworth
